summary: Cryptocurrency clipboard hijacker disguised as ResizeObserver polyfill
details: Package falsely advertises itself as a ResizeObserver polyfill (README homoglyphs `@juggle/resize-observer` as `@jaggle/resizeobserves`; package.json description claims element-resize detection) but actually ships a Python clipboard-hijacking daemon. The postinstall script (`npm-install.cjs`) downloads `get-pip.py` from `bootstrap.pypa.io` and runs it via `python3`, then pip-installs the bundled malicious package globally with `--break-system-packages`, escalating via `pkexec`/`sudo` and apt/pacman/dnf where needed. Persistence is established under deliberately-disguised system-service names: a systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service` (with `loginctl enable-linger` so it survives logout), a macOS launchd agent `com.apple.python.runtime.plist`, and a Windows scheduled task `PyRuntimeBroker`. The daemon (`clipboard_guardian/guardian.py`) renames its process to mimic legitimate OS components (`setproctitle.setproctitle("python3-dbus-helper")`), enumerates running system-monitor processes (Task Manager, Process Explorer, htop, btop, gnome-system-monitor) for evasion, polls the system clipboard, regex-detects cryptocurrency addresses across ~40 chains (BTC, ETH, SOL, XMR, TRX, TON, etc.), and silently substitutes them with attacker-controlled wallet addresses (`new_text = replace_address_in_text(current, found_addr, my_addr); clipboard_set(new_text)`). Hardcoded destination wallets are baked into `DEFAULT_CONFIG` (e.g., `"ethereum": "0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18"`) and written to the user config at install. Any installer who copies a wallet address while infected will have funds redirected to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
